title: The foreign exchange market
description: Practice what you have learned about exchange rates and the foreign exchange market in this exercise.
topic_path: Open economy: international trade and finance

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes what an exchange rate is in the foreign exchange market?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: How much of one currency it takes to buy another currency
The ratio of how much goods cost in one country relative to another country
The interest rate
The quantity of goods and services a currency can buy in other countries
The purchasing power of a currency domestically

assessment_items.question_content:
The graph of the foreign exchange market for the Nickelstan Nickel (NN) shown here is missing a label on the vertical axis. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**If the United States is a trading partner with Nickelstan, which of the following is the correct choice of a label for the vertical axis in this market?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A graph plotting supply and demand curves with the horizontal axis labeled "QNN" and the vertical axis labeled "ER1" shows a downward-sloping red line representing demand, labeled "DNN," and an upward-sloping green line representing supply, labeled "SNN." The two lines intersect at a point marked with a black dot, indicating the equilibrium quantity and price. Dashed lines extend horizontally and vertically from the equilibrium point to the axes.

choices.content: $ \$ $ per $NN$
$NN$ per $ \$ $
$NN$
Price
int. rate

assessment_items.question_content:
**In the foreign exchange market for the Mexican peso, which of the following would best describe the exchange rate for the peso?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The price of a peso in terms of some other currency
The price of a peso in terms of pesos
The interest rate earned from saving pesos
The interest given up by holding pesos
The price of a good in Mexico in terms of another goods in Mexico

assessment_items.question_content:
The currency of Thailand is the baht. 

**Which of the following would be an example of an appreciation of the baht?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The exchange rate changes from 2 rupees per baht to 3 rupees per baht
The exchange rate changed from 3 rupees per baht to 2 rupees per baht
The exchange rate changes from 10 baht per 1 dinar to 15 baht per 1 dinar
The exchange rate changed from 1 baht per 1 dollar to 1 baht per 0.5 dollars
The exchange rate changes from 100 baht per euro to 150 baht per euro

assessment_items.question_content:
The exchange rate of the Schrute buck ($SB$) to the Stanley nickel ($SN$) between 2016 and 2017 is given in the table below.

Year | $SB$ needed to buy $1SN$ | SN needed to buy  $1SB$
-|-|-
2016 | 5 | 0.2
2017 | 4 | 0.25

**What can we definitely say happened between 2016 and 2017?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The $SB$ appreciated
The $SB$ depreciated
The $SB$ can buy more goods domestically
The $SB$ can buy fewer goods domestically
The real interest rate in the country that uses the $SB$ decreased

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes depreciation of a currency?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It takes less of another currency to purchase that currency
It takes more of another currency to purchase that currency
Less money is needed to buy foreign goods
The domestic purchasing power of that currency has increased
The domestic purchasing power of that currency has decreased

assessment_items.question_content:
In 2017 the exchange rate for the Nickelstan Nickel ($NN$) was 20 Schrute Buck ($SB$) per $NN$. 
In 2018 the exchange rate for the Nickelstan Nickel ($NN$) was 30 Schrute Buck ($SB$) per $NN$. 

**Based on this information, which of the following statements must be true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The $SB$ depreciated
The $NN$ depreciated
Fewer people from other countries want to buy the NN
More people want to buy the SB
Both currencies have appreciated relative to each other.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes appreciation of a currency?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It takes more of another currency to purchase that currency
It takes less of another currency to purchase that currency
More of that currency is needed to buy foreign goods
The domestic purchasing power of that currency has increased
The domestic purchasing power of that currency has decreased

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following describes who benefits from a depreciation of the Japanese yen in international currency markets?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: South African residents visiting Japan
Japanese citizens wanting to buy German cars
Currency traders who hold large quantities of yen
A Japanese citizen living off a Japanese pension while retired in Belize
Japanese buyers of Bolivian government issued bonds

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is NOT a reason why citizens of the United States would want to buy the euro?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: For tourism travel to the United States from Europe
To buy European goods
To buy private financial assets in Europe
To buy public financial assets in Europe
For tourism travel to Europe from the United States

assessment_items.question_content:
Sheila lives in the United States and wants to buy Brian some chocolates imported from Belgium. The currency of the United States is the dollar; Belgium’s currency is the euro.

**How will the importation of chocolate from Belgium impact the market for the dollar and the market for the euro?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The supply of the dollar and the demand for the euro will increase.
The supply of the dollar will increase, and the demand for the euro will decrease.
The supply of the dollar and the demand for the euro will decrease.
There will be no change in the supply of the dollar, and the demand for the euro will decrease.
The supply of the dollar will decrease, and there will be no change in the demand for euros.

assessment_items.question_content:
The real interest rate in Hamsterville is higher than the real interest rate in Gatorland. Frank is a resident of Gatorland and wants to take advantage of the higher return in Hamsterville by buying financial assets from Hamsterville. The currency of Hamsterville is the snark, and the currency of Gatorland is the percy.

**What effect will the increase in demand for Hamsterville’s financial assets have on the market for the percy and the market for the snark?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The demand for the snark increases and the supply of the percy increases.
The demand for the snark will decrease, and the supply of the percy will increase.
The demand for the snark and the supply of the percy will decrease.
The demand for the snark and the supply of the percy will decrease.
There will be no change in the demand for the snark, and the supply of percies will increase.

assessment_items.question_content:
**How would Japan react to a depreciation of its own currency (the yen)?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The quantity of yen supplied would decrease
It would demand more of other currencies
The quantity of yen supplied would increase
It would buy more foreign financial assets
It would not react to a change in the value of the currency

assessment_items.question_content:
The currency of Hamsterville is the Hamsterville Snark ($SN$). 

**If citizens of Hamsterville want to buy U.S. goods, what happens in the market for the Snark and in the market for the U.S. dollar?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Hamsterville supplies snarks in the market for snarks; Hamsterville demands dollars in the  market for dollars
Hamsterville demands snarks in the market for snarks; Hamsterville supplies dollars in the market for dollars
No impact on the market for snarks; Hamsterville demands U.S. dollars in the market for dollars
No impact on the market for snarks; the United States supplies and demands dollars in the  market for dollars
Hamsterville supply snarks in the market for snarks; the United States demands dollars in the market for U.S. dollars

assessment_items.question_content:
The currency of Hamiltonia is the shot, and the currency of the United States is the dollar.

**If citizens of Hamiltonia want to take advantage of relatively higher interest rates in the United States, which of the following will happen?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The supply of the shot in the foreign exchange market for the shot will increase.
The demand for the dollar in the foreign exchange market for the dollar will decrease.
The demand for the shot in the foreign exchange market for the shot will increase.
The money supply in Hamiltonia will increase.
The money supply in Hamiltonia will decrease.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following describes an equilibrium in the foreign exchange market?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The quantity of a currency demanded is equal to the quantity of a currency supplied
One unit of a currency is valued at one unit of another currency
A currency continues to depreciate
A currency continues to appreciate
More of a currency is being supplied than people want to buy at the current exchange rate

assessment_items.question_content:
At the current exchange rate of the Korean won to the South African rand, South Africa is willing to supply 10 rand and Korea wants to buy 20 rand. Both countries have flexible exchange rates

**Which of the following would we expect to happen?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The won will depreciate
The demand for the rand will decrease
The rand will depreciate
The supply of the rand will decrease
The exchange rate will not change

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which term describes the situation in the foreign exchange market for the euro where the quantity demanded of the euro is equal to the quantity supplied of the euro?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Equilibrium
Depreciation
Shortage
Surplus
Appreciation

assessment_items.question_content:
The currency of Hamsterville is the snark, and the currency of Gatorland is the percy. At the current exchange rate, the quantity of Hamsterville snark demanded exceeds the quantity of Gatorland percies supplied.

**What will happen to the exchange rate of the snark and the exchange rate of the percy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The snark will appreciate; the percy will depreciate.
The snark will appreciate; there will be no change in the value of the percy.
The snark will depreciate; the percy will appreciate.
There will be no change in the value of either currency.
The snark will depreciate; the value of the percy will be unaffected.
